items.briefing:
A DigiCert survey has found that 81% of Australian organisations have already experienced AI-related security incidents or vulnerabilities, highlighting a growing and local threat. This comes as Australian leaders and a Nutanix study warn that AI adoption — particularly in the public sector — is moving faster than the governance and readiness frameworks needed to keep it safe. Compliance teams are also struggling to maintain visibility over how AI tools are being used within their organisations.

Separately, over one million emails have been observed using hidden text to trick AI-powered security filters, meaning some malicious emails may now be bypassing your organisation's defences undetected. **Practical tip:** Check with your IT team that your email security tools have been recently updated, and remind staff to stay cautious with unexpected emails even if they pass spam filters.

featured.title: DigiCert survey finds 81% of Australian organisations hit by AI security incidents or vulnerabilities
featured.quote: 81% of Australian organisations already hit by AI security incidents, showing urgent need for action.
